name: Fresh Asparagus Salad with Lemon Dijon Vinaigrette
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Lemon (for both juice and peel)
1 slice Bacon, cooked until crisp and crumbled coarsely
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring a large pot of water to a boil and blanch the asparagus (cook until tender but still bright green, 4-5 minutes). Remove with tongs and immediately plunge into ice-cold water to stop the cooking and preserve the color. Drain completely and arrange the asparagus on two salad plates.
2. Remove half the lemon peel and cut into extremely thin strips. Boil for 1-2 minutes; drain.
3. Squeeze 3 tablespoons juice from the lemon into a jar. Add the boiled peel, olive oil, salt, pepper, and Dijon mustard. Shake vigorously to combine. (This makes 3/4 cup dressing. Refrigerate the unused portion for future salads.)
4. At serving time, spoon some vinaigrette over the asparagus.
5. Sprinkle the crumbled bacon and pitted olives over the top, along with a grind of pepper.
Note: Salad per serving contains 58 calories, 4 grams protein, 6 grams carbohydrates, 2 grams fiber, 3 grams fat (0.7 grams saturated), and 134 milligrams sodium. Vinaigrette dressing per tablespoon contains 74 calories, 0 grams protein, 0.5 grams carbohydrates, 0 grams fiber, 8 grams fat (1 gram saturated), and 36 milligrams sodium.
